top_categories.workout_context: 62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Zadar's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
Gran Fondo Zadar's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Gran Fondo Zadar's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
At Gran Fondo Zadar, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Zadar. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Zadar. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Zadar, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: October in Zadar, Croatia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Gran Fondo Zadar. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Zadar’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Zadar. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Zadar, Croatia.
